No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Ruling. PLAINTIFF KATHLEEN PETREE'S Motion To Compel Further Verified Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One is taken OFF CALENDAR at the moving parties request. Judge Pro Tem: Tom Paoli; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-08-2017 Assigned Case Management

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-12-2017 Continued To May-31-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT to the sixth cause of action for fraud by omission is overruled. The order on the prior demurrer rejected all of Ford's arguments about the deficiency of the sixth cause of action except the statute of limitations argument. The court will not revisit those rulings. Plaintiff Kathleen Petree's additional allegations about American Pipe tolling are sufficient to withstand a demurrer. With those allegations, it cannot be determined as a matter of law from the face of the first amended complaint that the sixth cause of action is time-barred. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-17-2017 Continued To Apr-26-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-19-2016 Continued To Mar-08-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-16-2016 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rodney Gi of Strategic Legal Practices APC telephone number 310-929-4900 for Plaintiff Kathleen Petree.
Paul Efstratis of LeClair Ryan LLP telephone number 415-913-4957 for Defendant Ford Motor Company.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
Defendant Ford Motor Company's demurrer to the sixth cause of action for fraudulent concealment is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiff Kathleen Petree has adequately alleged the elements of a claim for fraudulent concealment that is not barred by the economic loss rule. However, the face of the complaint shows that the fraudulent concealment claim is time-barred unless the delayed discovery rule or some tolling doctrine applies. Ms. Petree has not alleged sufficient facts to invoke the delayed discovery rule or a tolling doctrine, but is given leave to do so, if she can do so in good faith. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and show compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Kelley Anne DeYoung, CSR #6780. (302/HK)
